files.year_idx_format: uint8_t[59986052]; entry i = (year - 1992) for the order of lineitem row i (0=1992..6=1998); all entries valid

storage_extensions.usage: mmap year_idx.bin as uint8_t[59986052]. In pass 2: l_year_arr[idx] gives year index directly — sequential access via sorted selvec. No l_orderkey column needed.

files.net_price_format: double[59986052]; net_price[i] = l_extendedprice[i] * (1.0 - l_discount[i]); all entries valid

storage_extensions.usage: mmap net_price.bin as double[59986052]. In pass 2: load l_net_price_arr[idx] instead of l_extprice[idx] * (1.0 - l_discount[idx]). Eliminates the l_discount column from pass 2, saving 173MB of unique cache lines at 5.5% qualifying density (36% of 480MB double-column CL touched). The amount formula becomes: amount = l_net_price_arr[idx] - ps_cost * l_quantity[idx].

join_order.notes: Parallel p_name scan (112MB, stride=56) to build uint8_t is_green[2000001] (2MB, L3-resident). Pass 1 reads only l_partkey (240MB sequential, parallel), stores qualifying {idx, lp} pairs in per-thread vectors.

Parallel scan of partsupp raw columns (128MB). CAS-based parallel insertion of ~440K green entries into compact hash (capacity 1048576=2^20, key=uint64 (partkey<<32|suppkey), value=double ps_supplycost, 16MB total — L3-resident). Probe during pass 2 using (lp<<32|ls) key constructed from selvec.lp and l_suppkey[idx].
uint8_t supp_nat[100001] (100KB, L1-resident). Single-threaded build from s_suppkey + s_nationkey columns.
char nation_names[25][26] from n_name.bin — 650 bytes, L1-resident.

scan.notes: Pass 1: parallel sequential scan of l_partkey only (240MB, no MADV_WILLNEED). Each thread stores qualifying {int32_t idx, int32_t lp} pairs in a local vector. OMP static schedule ensures per-thread sub-vectors are sorted; concatenation in thread-id order yields globally sorted result — no std::sort needed. Pass 2: divide sorted selvec evenly across nthreads. For each qualifying entry, use stored selvec[j].lp (avoids re-reading l_partkey), and software-prefetch all payload columns PREFETCH_DIST iterations ahead.

data_structures.part_filter: uint8_t is_green[2000001] — 2MB, L3-resident. Built in parallel from p_name scan.
data_structures.compact_ps_hash: Flat hash: uint64_t keys[1048576] (sentinel=0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF, CAS-claimed), double values[1048576]; 16MB total, L3-resident. Parallel CAS insertion of 440K green partsupp entries. key=(uint64(ps_partkey)<<32)|uint32(ps_suppkey).
data_structures.selection_vector: struct SelvecEntry { int32_t idx; int32_t lp; } per qualifying row (~3.3M entries = 26.4MB). Built per-thread with static OMP schedule (each thread's sub-vector sorted), concatenated in order — globally sorted, no std::sort needed.
data_structures.net_price_lookup: double l_net_price_arr[59986052] — mmap of lineitem.l_net_price extension. Random access via sorted selvec indices with software prefetch. Eliminates l_extendedprice and l_discount from pass 2.
data_structures.year_lookup: uint8_t l_year_arr[59986052] — mmap of lineitem.l_year_idx extension. Random access via sorted selvec indices with software prefetch.
data_structures.aggregation: double thread_agg[25][7] per thread (1400 bytes each, cache-line aligned with alignas(64) padding between nation rows). Merged into final_agg after pass 2.

parallelism.notes: Phase 1 (p_name + partsupp scans): nthreads parallel. Phase 2 (pass 1): nthreads parallel static OMP over 60M rows. Phase 3 (pass 2): divide selvec evenly across nthreads — each thread handles a contiguous slice of sorted indices for ascending cache access. Software prefetch distance: PREFETCH_DIST=32 iterations ahead for all 4 payload columns simultaneously.

optimization_notes: Three root causes addressed. (1) Category C: data_loading exclusive was 47ms due to MADV_WILLNEED on l_partkey (240MB) — the kernel walks 60K page-table entries even for hot-cache data at ~0.7µs/page = 42ms overhead. Fix: remove l_partkey.prefetch() entirely; the parallel OMP pass-1 scan naturally faults pages in parallel across 64 threads (~1ms added to pass 1 vs 42ms saved). (2) Category E: pass 2 accessed 3 double payload columns (l_extprice, l_discount, l_quantity) each causing ~173MB of unique cache-line fetches at 5.5% qualifying density. New lineitem.l_net_price column version (double[60M], built in 0.45s) precomputes l_extprice*(1-l_discount), replacing two column reads with one — saving 173MB of unique CL from l_discount. (3) Category B: pass 2 was latency-bound (~25GB/s effective) due to irregular-stride random access into 480MB columns; hardware prefetcher cannot detect the variable stride. Fix: explicit software prefetching with PREFETCH_DIST=32 ahead for all 4 payload columns in the pass-2 loop, converting to bandwidth-bound at ~50GB/s. Additionally, selection vector now stores (idx, lp) pairs so l_partkey need not be re-read in pass 2 (saves 142MB unique CL). Budget: data_loading 7ms + dim_filter 5ms + build_joins 9ms + pass1 5ms + pass2 11ms = 37ms target.
